mentions.note: Mentioned as long-term winner in Magnificent 7 / Fang+ portfolio; facing multiple compression and investor rotation to storage plays but Cramer maintains conviction on mega-cap tech reflow.
Enterprise software suffering irrational multiple compression from AI platform fears; no actual earnings misses; money rotating to storage plays; wait for stabilization.
Historic Cramer Fang+ holding; facing temporary money outflows to storage plays but Cramer believes capital will return to Magnificent 7 as storage rally peaks.

Private company; Cramer mentioned he has seen rival AI products created at fraction of cost using Anthropic's platform; not enough conviction to recommend but technology competitive threat to enterprise software vendors.
Known as 'Dr. Colossus' of semiconductor; acting really badly; part of broader Mag 7 pressure as money rotates to storage plays; watch for reversion.

Cramer thesis: bought relentlessly on the way down (50%, 60%, 70% declines) because management promised cash flow would be higher than expected; followed the cash flow metric, not headlines; stock made new high today; psychology of market had written off Boeing but fundamentals support recovery.
Optical electronics / connectivity solutions player for AI buildout; stock has rallied hard (banshee-like move) but technology is really good and a winner; use any dip to buy more stock.
Enterprise software suffering extreme P/E multiple shrinkage on irrational AI disruption fears despite no earnings misses; momentum money fleeing to storage; psychological, not fundamental.
Caller Jim asked about IPO energy company (Venture Global mentioned); Cramer clarified he does not like Venture Global—did not deliver and won't deliver at all; no coverage provided for Carpenter.

Perfect execution by CEO Larry Culp but market penalizes A-student getting more A's (wants A+); every line beat including margin improvement yet stock down 7.4%; psychology of 'too loved' leading to selloff despite perfection.
Do not like despite infrastructure expansion and resolved lawsuits; company didn't deliver and won't deliver; skip it.
Only Magnificent 7 stock currently strong; Cramer expects money to flow back to most of the seven eventually because they have too many levers and too much capital to bet against.
Up 40% YTD despite weak quarter; demand for chips insane; CEO Pat Gelsinger has more up his sleeve per Cramer; do not give up on this stock—mistake to abandon it now.

Caller Jim asks about Kraft (merged 2011); notes Warren Buffett divesting 28% stake; Cramer says new CEO Steve Carell came from Kellogg is okay, but he and Ben Stole can't get behind it—not enough good happening in food business; Cramer is a seller, not a buyer.
Caller deep in red (bought 2021); Cramer advises to take the loss and get out; notes Rivian at 16 is better if you want EV exposure (though Cramer doesn't like either).
Take the loss; company deep underwater; Rivian at $16 better positioned if want EV exposure (though Cramer doesn't like either).
P/E multiple contracted from 30x to 22x earnings; market skeptical of growth rate; Cramer says Meta has 'lost its way' amid OpenAI competition and generative AI platform threats.

Turnaround story accelerating under CEO Bill Brown (ex-L3 Harris); organic growth turned positive (+2.1% in 2025), operating margins recovering to 23%, EPS +18% YTD; 12 straight quarterly beats; new product launches up 68% (284 products in 2025); stock down 8% from Dec high on overreaction to mixed quarter; valued at just under 19x forward P/E—cheap; Cramer rarely pounds table like this; time to buy into a great contrarian entry point for the first time.
Magnificent 7 member; money temporarily flowing to storage plays but company has too much cash and smart leadership; patience needed.
Storage play up 39% YTD; memory shortage supporting extended rally; devices like gasoline—no price resistance due to AI data center demand; Cramer sees further upside even at current levels.
Caller asked; Cramer said he doesn't know and something crazy is going on; needs to do work before opining; doesn't want to 'cuff it' (make hasty judgment).
Fang+ original constituent; suffering P/E compression like Mag 7 peers; Cramer expects eventual reflow once storage rotation exhausts.
Steel peer mentioned alongside Steel Dynamics as beneficiary of US strength and tariff support; Cramer likes the two of them together.

Part of Magnificent 7 facing P/E multiple compression (from 30x to 22x) as money rotates to storage plays; Cramer owns in travel trust but expects eventual reflow of capital back to mega-cap tech leaders.

Rallied 3% on weak Q4 quarter (flat organic sales vs. 5% expected, baby/feminine down 4%) due to crushed expectations and new CEO Shilesh Dejurikar credibility; international strength (Brazil +8%, China +3%) and innovation (284 new products, +68% YTD) suggest turnaround; stock bounced from $137 Jan 7 low to $150; Cramer bought for charitable trust at $146 and $141; more room to run.
Cramer likes it and momentum is not going away; however, storage plays (Seagate, SanDisk, Micron) are taking turn right now with all the money coming in; warn not to overstay welcome.
At $16 level; Cramer says if you like new EV makers, Rivian is the one to be in (better than Lucid), but Cramer doesn't personally like either.

Caller bought in November and asking for take; stock on upswing; Cramer loves the step-function model and the whole business (fund biotech, get returns); terrific company; liked it when GA brought it public and likes it now.
Storage play up 112% YTD in remarkable rally; underinvested for years, caught flatfooted by AI explosion; now raising prices repeatedly with no resistance; galloping to where it has to go.
Caller Mike down 60%, now only down 5%, worried about California regulations; Cramer says not a great oil services company, not a great oil company—stay away, get out.

Stock down 40% from Aug high despite strong fundamentals; provides data, odds, betting tools for sports industry (picks-and-shovels model); CEO sees Winter Olympics (NHL) and World Cup 2026 (48 teams, 100+ matches) as huge catalysts; California, Florida, Texas represent >50% of US GDP opportunity; Cramer thinks stock too cheap and belongs higher; waiting to see if 2026 event volumes drive analyst recognition.
Excellent company; America doing well and tariffs working; caller owned it for couple years with great returns; Cramer calls it a terrific buy.
Storage disc drive play up 26% YTD on AI-driven memory demand; beneficiary of commodity shortage; money rotating from Mag 7 into these hot names.
Enterprise software multiple compression on AI disruption fears; no earnings misses; psychological selling driving rotation to storage plays.

Caller Garrett owns ~300 shares; Cramer says hold on to it; new CEO, 4% yield, bouncing off lows for first time in long time; willing to bless owning it, maybe even buy another 100 if it hits $100; wants to have new CEO on show.
Part of Magnificent 7; experiencing P/E compression like peers but company too strong to bet against long-term; stay the course.

Caller Jim (closing call) bullish on energy expansion and contract stability; Cramer said he and Ben Stole kicked it around but decided they couldn't get behind it; did a good piece recently explaining they just didn't deliver and won't deliver; no conviction in the thesis.
Enterprise software with extreme P/E shrinkage on generative AI disruption fears; no misses but market psychology negative; avoid until clarity emerges.
Storage play up 41% YTD; benefiting from memory shortage and AI data center buildout; Cramer sees momentum continuing as capital flows into commodity storage.
